title:
@KSimback: A few tips to 3-5x your Claude usage within plan limits

If you’re like me and y...
rawContent:
Tweet by @KSimback (Kevin Simback 🍷)
Author bio: COO @delphi_labs - building + investing in crypto and AI. Ex @IBM, @McKinsey, @CarnegieMellon, reformed CFA
Text: A few tips to 3-5x your Claude usage within plan limits

If you’re like me and you’ve hit Claude limits over the past few weeks, here’s what you can do:

For general use:

1. Prompt better (see article - multiple tips)

2. Do the “early morning hello” trick to start your 5-hour window 1-2 hours before you begin work, can use tools like freshquota or claude-warmup to automate it

3. Install plugins or use a dashboard to show usage limits (check out ccburn) and use /compact regularly

For coding:

1. Use a .claudeignore like .gitignore - can get aggressive with this, some saving 50-70% with this alone

2. Lean out you CLAUDE[.]md to avoid bloated context reloads

3. Queue up heavy tasks to run towards the end of usage windows where you have use remaining

4. Lifecycle hooks + custom skills - if you’re doing repeated tasks, optimize them for fewer tokens

For even more, go multi-model - yes you need to pay a bit more for other plans but start offloading activities to Codex or other model of choice

Don’t make Claude do everything (research, plan, code, review) and begin to orchestrate across models
